title: ceratophytum tobagense
intro: Ceratophytum tobagense, a rare and endemic plant species found in the island of São Tomé and Principe, is a succulent plant belonging to the family Campanulaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. It is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, which are often used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential medicinal applications.
